chief_complaint: An 86-year-old man with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) presents with acute respiratory failure due to severe pneumonia.
history: The patient has been managed since 2018 with inhaled corticosteroids, long-acting muscarinic antagonists, long-acting beta-agonists, and roflumilast. He was admitted to the medical intensive-care unit with worsening condition despite treatment with piperacillin/tazobactam and methylprednisolone.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not explicitly stated.
labs: Imaging revealed left lower lobe consolidation. Sputum samples identified Nocardia species, confirming pulmonary nocardiosis.
